title: When Natural Disasters Strike, Another Crisis Hits Those Recovering From Opioid Addiction
summary: Using Hurricane Helene as a teachable moment, a group of doctors outlined concrete steps that lawmakers can take to reverse a crisis in getting substance use medications during natural disasters.

title: States Rush To Figure Out How To Enforce Trump’s Medicaid Work Requirements
summary: A KFF survey of state Medicaid officials offers insight into lingering uncertainty and differing plans for work requirement implementation as the Jan. 1 deadline approaches.

title: Saving Lives by Changing Lives: The Next Frontier in Suicide Prevention
summary: Someone in America dies by suicide every 11 minutes. It’s a tragic and entrenched problem. A new approach to prevention shifts the focus from stopping harm in moments of crisis to upstream policies that give people reasons to live.

title: Trump’s Medicaid Work Mandate Debuting in Nebraska to Much Dismay
summary: On May 1, the state will become the first to require people on the government health program to fulfill a work requirement or lose their coverage under a new rule that was a key part of congressional Republicans’ One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
summary: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

title: An Urgent Care Treated Her Allergic Reaction. An ER Monitored Her — For $6,700.
summary: A bug bite and an allergic reaction ultimately sent a North Carolina woman to the emergency room, where she had a couple of brief chats with a doctor and a dose of medicine. Now she questions why the charges were so high.

title: Big Companies Position Themselves for Payday From $50B Federal Rural Health Fund
summary: A $50 billion federal fund is supposed to modernize rural health with electronic health records, AI, telehealth, and more. But community clinics and rural health advocates fear that the contractors administering the money for states will bite off a big chunk before it reaches rural patients.

title: Florida Delays Children’s Health Insurance Expansion as Uninsured Rate Rises
summary: Florida’s KidCare expansion has been stuck in legal limbo since February 2024. Since then, the number of uninsured children in Florida has risen to 400,000 — one of the highest state tallies.

title: The Help That Many Older Americans Need Most
summary: With shortages of medical professionals and an aging population, thousands of community healthcare workers prevent older adults from falling through the cracks.

title: Gounder Culls the News, From Ticks and AI to Who Might Lead CDC
summary: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national or local media recently to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.
